item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved the article but it needs more substance
Like it left me with many questions
Can foundations members buy MOTH HELM?
Does the moth have infinite MOTH HELM?
Who is allowed to contact him?
Otherwise great article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THE WEB DESIGN POWERS OF MOTH HELM PROVIDE UPDATED CSS GUARANTEED COMPATIBLE WITH MOST MAJOR BROWSERS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's with the sudden change in the design of the page?! That really freaked me out and only happens in this skip. I seriously thought it was an effect of the damn thing.

item.title: Anyone know where I might get a moth helm?
item.content:encoded: I need a moth helm for various reasons. Please help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really liked it! Yeah, there are lots of talking animals on here, but nothing as cute and creative as this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a liiiiiiiittle bit too silly for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took use of the lämp meme didn’t you, dj?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like these quirky SCPs a lot more than the apocalyptic horror ones. Also short and to the point - sometimes brevity is a virtue, not everything needs an epic backstory. Easy upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could have heard more about the unrivaled power of the MOTH HELM. He's such a wholesome salesman and I hope this gets updated with more interviews!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this one! I’d buy a helmet from him. Have an upvote.
Also, it reminded me of this: https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=erc7gwnSf38
And I’m kind of angry because I can’t unsee it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oldie but goldie
i remembered this existed and that i hadn't upvoted it yet!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was absolutely adorable, and just the palate cleanser I needed after reading something eldritch like 2480.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely +1, tiny moth helmets and trying to explain why moths always seem to aim for the face, that's funny

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed and heard it as a way too exited salemanmoth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly really like that the only information you can get out of it is incredibly boring and not at all useful (seeing as how it never reveals where to send the payments, who is making these helmets, any of its origin beyond getting the helmet, etc).

I know it makes for a creepier/more intense story when the anomalous being can talk and what it says is deeply important and fascinating, and possibly about the fate of the world, but it's much more realistic that a significant number of them would be annoying and a waste of time. I like the idea of researchers having to wade through hundreds of anomalous talking animals who are complete idiots in order to find the one that Knows Something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The discovery is cliche; I wish more effort was put into justifying that weird coincidence. Besides that, though, I enjoyed this. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interviews where the entity says "Doctor" always bug me

Heh. Bug

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed. This reminds me of the toaster from Red Dwarf. +7

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2598 pauses for twenty seconds, is observed spinning counterclockwise around its containment cell light.

I happened to stumble upon this article again and felt like reading it again, because that's the beauty of an interesting article, it's fun to read many times over. Somehow I missed this line, since I don't remember reading it.

Kudos to the author, because it really seems like you did some in-the-field research on moth behavior. Frickin moths and their erratic movement patterns. I swear the last time I went camping all the moths that landed on the lantern were doing the hokey-pokey. :|

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Dear lord.
It's happening.
How did I forget to vote this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know how to fix the rating module, so I think I'll just bump and say that the rating module is still wonky for this article.

E: Problem appears fixed now. Thanks wikidot for making me look dumb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I told myself that I wouldn't upvote another talking animal, and I was right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2598 is able to communicate with humans by colliding with their heads

Heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incidentally, what's this thing's lifespan? Does it have anomalous longevity?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As quirky as it may be, the existence of this thing implies a lot. And by "a lot," I mean an entire society of sentient moths that are aggressive capitalists and bad salespeople. I mean, if we're containing one, and it has no contact with the outside world, yet it still makes its sales pitch, then either it's a relatively dumb moth, or something seriously weird is going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still think a -J suits this article more. Anyway, upvote! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2598: I WAS ONCE LIKE YOU DOCTOR

Interviews where the entity says "Doctor" always bug me. Just seems, too formal for most of them?

"Doc! Doc! Let me tell you about this dream I had. It was an out of body, well, out of head experience, let tell you!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this could be a lot better if you included something about what happens if you actually purchase a MOTH HELM

+1, though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The story is nice, but the item itself lacks content. Overall a fun read for a layman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Step 1: Buy Moth Helms
Step 2: Put Moth Helms on SCP-408
Step 3: …
Step 4: PROFIT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EXACTLY my complaint.
Logic dictates BUYING one of those, and then trying to fit it on a regular moth!
It's just obvios!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This post doesn't add anything to the conversation. While you're entitled to your opinion, you're also being an asshole, and you should take a step back and think about whether or not what you're going to type out is helpful. Constructive criticism is great. Pointless criticism is useless, and only lowers the standard of discourse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, if you're satisfied with that standard, probably not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like humor that knows when the impact has been made and then gets out. +1 moth helm is hilarious.

Here's my question: what happens when they agree to purchase MOTH HELM? Surely the Foundation would try and see if they could discover its manufacturer this way or at the very least learn more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was STUPID. Now take my + upvote and three payments of $19.99.

Seriously though, it was silly and it made me laugh. I sort of like how short it was. It didn't overstay its welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yissssssssssssssssssssssss

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh my god college moth bros
gotta write a tale with some Greek life moths
collab sometime in the future can you dig it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bunch of them have gotten into our office at work, and usually spend their time bro-ing out in the hallways. It's not until night when they start going crazy, which I assume means that they're active ravers and their head bumps are an invitation to dance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
can butterflies order moth helm because I am running out of stocking stuffer ideas

This made me grin. It does feel a little on the short side, but I have my share of crazy moths near my house, and it made me reconsider my perspective of them. Some of the larger ones cluster on my window screen and keep me company during late study hours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am now compelled to declare that I am doing things "for the glory of Moth Helm".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. Not until just now, no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You ever read this before?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 30 out of 2000+? That doesn't sound too bad at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another talking animal. And what it has to say just so happens to be something toootally waaacky.

Sounds familiar. Probably from every single Disney/Pixar movie ever made. And what by now is surely at least 30 SCP articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I reached that point a while ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed it, though there has been a noticeable trend of slightly anomalous yet quirky articles as of late.

Upvoted for MOTH HELM

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I AM GLAD TO HEAR THAT YOU HAVE BEEN PERSUADED INTO THE RIGHTEOUSNESS OF MOTH HELM NO WORRIES SIR REPRESENTATIVE WILL ARRIVE SOON

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You will respect the power of MOTH HELM.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's been a moth living in the grating of my computer near my fan where the computer lights are, I fear he may be mentally disturbed or otherwise impaired, whom might I contact to acquire a moth helm for this gentlemoth?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
apart from its anomalous characteristics, appears no different from other moths of the same species, aside from a small helmet it constantly wears.

Seems awkward to me here. Apart from and aside from in the same sentence? I think maybe the apart from clause (it's a clause right?) can be removed, I'd say the helmet is an anomalous characteristic anyway =)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the last time I upvote a dumb animal that talks funny, I swear to god.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kind of stupid. It's yet another (sorta) talking animal, and the commercial joke isn't really enough to make it interesting. I smiled, slightly, but that's not enough to save this from a downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know that the moth's "speech" is in all caps because it's a Morse code transcription, but I couldn't help but read it as a loud voice like it was a car salesman or Billy Mays.

"YES THIS IS THE FINEST MOTH HELM YOU CAN FIND ON THE MARKET. BUY NOW WHILE YOU CAN. SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did find this somewhat amusing, but to be honest, it seemed like it ended too quickly. I feel that there could have been more. It was funny, but it was pretty bland. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I saw the picture I took that to mean that Zyn had written up another article.
Then it turned out to be a moth, which was a pleasant surprise.

The article itself is very short and lacks substance, but it brought a smile to my face.

EDIT: Also italicized the species name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So you know when you sit under a porch light and moths divebomb you but seemingly ignore the rest of your body and aim directly for your face?

What if they're just trying to sell you something?

Anyway, much thanks to Piefish, Chubert, Crayne, and anybody else who happened to glance at this in chat! Your opinions and critiques were, as always, invaluable.
